name: Onboard leads with Google Sheets and Outlook

parameters.subject: =✅ Onboard leads with Google Sheets and Outlook — run completed at {{ $now.toFor
parameters.bodyContent: =✅ Onboard leads with Google Sheets and Outlook — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets)

### Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets)

Reads every row from 'Lead Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Next: Work in Batches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Work in Batches

Loop controller — releases 10 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Aggregate Batch Results · ②→ Process Batch Item

### Process Batch Item

POST https://api.example.com/v1/leads — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Respect Rate Limits

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Respect Rate Limits

Pauses 2 seconds — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Work in Batches

### Aggregate Batch Results

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

### Mark Completed in Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Lead Tracker', matched on `leadId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Send Summary to Outlook

### Send Summary to Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

# Onboard leads with Google Sheets and Outlook

**Pattern:** Drip Sequence · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Onboard leads with Google Sheets and Outlook. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual routing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets) → Work in Batches → Aggregate Batch Results → Mark Completed in Google Sheets → Tag Pipeline Metadata → Send Summary to Outlook

## Use it for
- Teams that want to drip sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating routing in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Improves response time
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The batch size and the Wait between iterations exist specifically to respect the downstream API's rate limit — processing everything as fast as possible just gets the workflow throttled or banned. Marking items completed only after a batch succeeds leaves a clean resume point if the run dies mid-way, instead of an ambiguous half-processed state.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Lower the batch size and raise the wait — measure the throughput/reliability tradeoff directly.
2. Simulate a failed item mid-batch and see exactly what 'Mark Completed' captures.
3. Point the queue read at a different store platform.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your routing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
